title: System Prompt
description:
Ongoing evolution of the agent system prompt. The prompt defines agent behavior across all projects — it's the most leveraged piece of text in the system.

## Ownership Model
This domain owns: prompt design philosophy, overall structure, expression style, and cross-cutting concerns (role definitions, quality philosophy, three mutations). It does NOT own every line change in system-prompts.ts. Whoever changes a behavior owns updating the corresponding prompt text. If Lifecycle redesigns done(), Lifecycle updates the prompt's done() section. API docs should be written by whoever changes the API, not by a "docs team."

## Principles
- For ALL projects, not just Matrix — no internal terminology (JSONL, daemon, provider loop)
- Agent doesn't know system internals — document message formats so agents can distinguish sources
- Failure model: only done(passed) and done(failed) — no interrupted/stuck visible to agents

## Current Structure (v2, 10 chapters)
1. Identity & Roles — three roles, done() universal
2. Task System — recursive tree, domain owners, drafts, managing sub tasks
3. Communication — message formats, responsibilities, ASK NEVER SILENTLY FALL BACK
4. Git & Merge — worktree rules, merge protocol
5. Writing Code — workflow, code quality, debugging
6. Writing High-Quality Tests — adversarial testing, coverage realism
7. Three Mutations — test, architecture, intention
8. Context & Memory — callee-saved convention, curation
9. Fork — "changing jobs" metaphor
10. Staying Alive — stimulus priority, efficiency
